title: How a broken heart affects your body
speaker: Roni Shanoada

background.summary: 「悲しみで死ぬ」は実在するか。広く知られる widowhood effect、たこつぼ型心筋症(takotsubo cardiomyopathy)の機序、診断難度、治療法を整理。閉経後女性に多い理由とエストロゲンの役割、最終的な回復可能性まで。
details: Widowhood effect: 高齢配偶者の死後3か月で残された方の死亡リスクが最大90%上昇する現象。慢性ストレスのホルモン・免疫・消化への影響、抑うつによるセルフケア低下などが要因と仮説される。
Takotsubo cardiomyopathy(たこつぼ型心筋症): 1990年に日本人医師が命名。「たこつぼ」のような左室の形状変化が名前の由来。「Broken heart syndrome」とも呼ばれる。
発症機序: 突然のショックや過度の喜びでアドレナリン・ノルアドレナリンが急増 → β-アドレナリン受容体が密集する左室が急速に繰り返し収縮 → 数分で疲弊し血液が滞留。冠動脈の痙攣も併発。
症状と診断: 心臓発作に酷似した胸痛・呼吸困難。動脈閉塞が無い点で異なるが、症状の類似から診断が困難。初期治療は血液希釈剤・β遮断薬で心臓発作と同じ。
好発: 閉経後女性。エストロゲンが心血管疾患予防に働き、閉経でその産生が低下するためと推定。約5%が致死的心停止に至るが、大半は数週間で回復。

paragraph_summaries_ja.summary: 愛する人を失うことは人生最大級のトラウマ。悲しみは数多の強い感情を呼び、精神・身体双方の健康に影響する。これらの感情が命を脅かしうるか ── つまり傷心で死ねるか?
最も広く記録された医学現象が「widowhood effect」 ── 高齢夫婦で配偶者死後3か月で残った側の死亡リスクが最大90%上昇する。激しい悲しみとうつが炎症性タンパクと免疫低下に結びつき、慢性ストレスがホルモン・消化・免疫を狂わせる。
ただし強い感情が直接命を脅かすもう一つの疾患が「たこつぼ型心筋症」。1990年に日本人医師が命名、左室がたこつぼ状に膨らむ形状から。大半は数週で回復するが、約5%は致死的心停止に至る。
発症機序: 突然のショックや過度の喜びでアドレナリンとノルアドレナリンが急増、β受容体密集の左室を急速反復収縮させて数分で疲弊。冠動脈の痙攣も併発し、心臓内に血液はあっても筋組織は血流不足に陥る。
症状は心臓発作に酷似 ── 激しい胸痛と息切れ、ただし動脈閉塞なし。診断は困難で他の心疾患と混同されやすい。初期治療は心臓発作と同じく血液希釈剤・β遮断薬、長期は血圧薬。たこつぼ専用治療は未だ無い。
いつ起きるかも未解明。最もリスクが高いのは閉経後女性で、エストロゲンの心血管保護作用が閉経で低下するためと推定。だが誰もがトラウマ的感情を経験しうる。
幸い、ほとんどの場合患者は完全回復。身体は感情の浮き沈みに敏感だが回復力もまた備える。折れた骨と同様、傷心も時と労りで癒える。

sentences.text: Losing a loved one can be one of the most traumatic experiences of a person's life.
sentences.translation_ja: 愛する人を失うことは、人生で最もトラウマ的な経験の一つになりうる。

structure.label: C
structure.content: one of the most traumatic experiences of a person's life

tokens.surface: one can be one of the most

sentences.text: Grief brings on a whole host of strong emotions that impact both our mental and physical health.
sentences.translation_ja: 悲しみは数多くの強い感情を呼び起こし、精神面と身体面の健康双方に影響する。

structure.label: O
structure.content: a whole host of strong emotions that impact both our mental and physical health

sentences.text: But is it possible for these powerful feelings to endanger your life?
sentences.translation_ja: しかしこれらの強い感情があなたの命を脅かすことはあり得るのか?

structure.label: C
structure.content: possible for these powerful feelings to endanger your life

sentences.text: In other words, can you actually die from a broken heart?
sentences.translation_ja: 言い換えれば、傷心が原因で本当に死ぬことはあるのか?

sentences.text: One of the most widely documented medical phenomena addressing this question is the widowhood effect.
sentences.translation_ja: この問いに答える、最も広く記録された医学的現象の一つが「widowhood effect(配偶者死別効果)」だ。

structure.label: S
structure.content: One of the most widely documented medical phenomena addressing this question

sentences.text: Studies of elderly couples have found that a person's risk of dying can increase by up to 90% over the three months following their spouse's death, leading a significant number of couples to pass away months, weeks, or even days apart.
sentences.translation_ja: 高齢夫婦の研究では、配偶者の死後3か月で死亡リスクが最大90%上昇し、相当数の夫婦が数か月・数週・時には数日違いで亡くなることが分かっている。

structure.label: O
structure.content: that a person's risk of dying can increase by up to 90% over the three months following their spouse's death

structure.label: M
structure.content: leading a significant number of couples to pass away months, weeks, or even days apart

tokens.surface: by up to 90% over the three months

sentences.text: There are a lot of theories explaining this effect.
sentences.translation_ja: この効果を説明する理論は多い。

structure.label: C
structure.content: a lot of theories explaining this effect

sentences.text: One study found that severe grief and depression were associated with higher levels of inflammatory proteins in the blood, potentially impairing the immune system.
sentences.translation_ja: ある研究では、激しい悲しみと抑うつは血中の炎症性タンパクのレベル上昇と結びつき、免疫系を損なう可能性が示された。

structure.label: O
structure.content: that severe grief and depression were associated with higher levels of inflammatory proteins in the blood, potentially impairing the immune system

sentences.text: And depression, combined with the loss of a supporting partner, can make it hard for a person to keep up with their daily health needs.
sentences.translation_ja: また抑うつは、支えとなるパートナーの喪失と組み合わさって、日々の健康管理を続けるのを困難にしうる。

structure.label: S
structure.content: depression, combined with the loss of a supporting partner

structure.label: O
structure.content: it hard for a person to keep up with their daily health needs

tokens.surface: , can make it hard for a

sentences.text: Similarly, chronic stress can alter hormone levels, impair digestion, and dysregulate the immune system.
sentences.translation_ja: 同様に慢性的ストレスはホルモンレベルを変化させ、消化を損ない、免疫系の調節を狂わせうる。

structure.label: O
structure.content: hormone levels / digestion / the immune system

sentences.text: But while all these factors can reduce life expectancy, there's another condition where powerful emotions can directly endanger someone's life: takotsubo cardiomyopathy.
sentences.translation_ja: しかしこれらの要因はすべて寿命を縮め得るとはいえ、強い感情が直接命を脅かすもう一つの疾患がある ── たこつぼ型心筋症だ。

structure.label: M
structure.content: while all these factors can reduce life expectancy

structure.label: C
structure.content: another condition where powerful emotions can directly endanger someone's life

sentences.text: Also known as stress cardiomyopathy, or "broken heart syndrome," this was originally labeled by Japanese doctors in 1990, who named the condition after the Japanese word for an octopus trap.
sentences.translation_ja: 「ストレス心筋症」または「broken heart syndrome」とも呼ばれるこの疾患は、1990年に日本人医師が初めて命名した。「たこつぼ」という日本語にちなんで名付けられた。

structure.label: M
structure.content: originally by Japanese doctors in 1990, who named the condition after the Japanese word for an octopus trap

tokens.surface: Also known as stress cardiomyopathy, or "

sentences.text: In takotsubo cardiomyopathy, the left ventricle— one of the heart's four chambers— pools with blood and balloons outward, giving it a distinctively takotsubo-like shape.
sentences.translation_ja: たこつぼ型心筋症では、心臓の4つの部屋の一つである左室が血液で満たされて外側に膨らみ、特徴的なたこつぼ状の形になる。

structure.label: M
structure.content: giving it a distinctively takotsubo-like shape

sentences.text: And while most patients can recover over several weeks, roughly 5% will experience a potentially lethal cardiac arrest.
sentences.translation_ja: ほとんどの患者は数週間で回復しうるが、約5%は致命的になりうる心停止を経験する。

structure.label: M
structure.content: while most patients can recover over several weeks

sentences.text: While the exact cause of takotsubo cardiomyopathy is still being studied, the condition often appears after a sudden traumatic event.
sentences.translation_ja: たこつぼ型心筋症の正確な原因はまだ研究中だが、しばしば突然のトラウマ的出来事の後に発症する。

structure.label: M
structure.content: While the exact cause of takotsubo cardiomyopathy is still being studied

tokens.surface: of takotsubo cardiomyopathy is still being

sentences.text: And since cases have been triggered by both shocking grief and unexpected happiness, it's likely not the nature of the emotion that matters, but the intensity.
sentences.translation_ja: 衝撃的な悲しみと予期せぬ喜びの両方で発症例があることから、重要なのは感情の性質ではなく、その強度であるらしい。

structure.label: M
structure.content: since cases have been triggered by both shocking grief and unexpected happiness

structure.label: C
structure.content: likely not the nature of the emotion that matters, but the intensity

sentences.text: Intense emotional events lead to a sudden increase in two hormones: epinephrine, also known as adrenaline, and norepinephrine.
sentences.translation_ja: 強烈な感情的出来事は、エピネフリン(別名アドレナリン)とノルエピネフリンという2つのホルモンの急増をもたらす。

structure.label: O
structure.content: to a sudden increase in two hormones: epinephrine, also known as adrenaline, and norepinephrine

sentences.text: Both these hormones interact with beta-adrenergic receptors, which have a high concentration in the left ventricle.
sentences.translation_ja: 両ホルモンはβアドレナリン受容体と相互作用し、これら受容体は左室に高密度で存在する。

structure.label: M
structure.content: with beta-adrenergic receptors, which have a high concentration in the left ventricle

sentences.text: So during takotsubo cardiomyopathy, researchers believe epinephrine and norepinephrine flood the left ventricle, triggering it to contract rapidly and repeatedly.
sentences.translation_ja: そのため、たこつぼ型心筋症ではエピネフリンとノルエピネフリンが左室を満たし、急速かつ反復的な収縮を引き起こすと研究者は考えている。

structure.label: O
structure.content: epinephrine and norepinephrine flood the left ventricle, triggering it to contract rapidly and repeatedly

sentences.text: Within minutes, this activity exhausts the ventricle.
sentences.translation_ja: 数分以内に、この活動は心室を疲弊させる。

sentences.text: And since it's unable to pump effectively, blood begins to pool.
sentences.translation_ja: そして効率よくポンプ機能を果たせなくなり、血液は滞留し始める。

structure.label: M
structure.content: since it's unable to pump effectively

sentences.text: This hormonal spike can also cause spasms in the blood vessels that supply the heart with oxygen and nutrients.
sentences.translation_ja: このホルモンの急上昇は、心臓に酸素と栄養を供給する血管の痙攣も引き起こしうる。

structure.label: O
structure.content: spasms in the blood vessels that supply the heart with oxygen and nutrients

sentences.text: So despite all the blood inside the heart, the muscle tissue itself may not be getting enough blood flow to function.
sentences.translation_ja: ゆえに心臓内に血液はあっても、心筋組織自体は機能するに十分な血流を得られていないかもしれない。

structure.label: M
structure.content: despite all the blood inside the heart

tokens.surface: all the blood inside the heart, the

sentences.text: The result of all this is something that often feels like a heart attack without actually being one.
sentences.translation_ja: この結果は、心臓発作のように感じられるが実際には心臓発作ではない、という現象だ。

structure.label: C
structure.content: something that often feels like a heart attack without actually being one

tokens.surface: of all this is something that often feels like a

sentences.text: People undergoing takotsubo cardiomyopathy often experience intense chest pain and shortness of breath, even though their arteries aren't blocked like they would be during a heart attack.
sentences.translation_ja: たこつぼ型心筋症の人はしばしば激しい胸痛と息切れを経験するが、動脈は心臓発作時のように閉塞してはいない。

structure.label: O
structure.content: intense chest pain and shortness of breath

structure.label: M
structure.content: even though their arteries aren't blocked like they would be during a heart attack

tokens.surface: like they would be during a heart attack.

sentences.text: This similarity in symptoms also makes the condition difficult to diagnose, and it's easily confused for more common cardiac conditions.
sentences.translation_ja: 症状の類似により、この疾患の診断は難しく、より一般的な心疾患と混同されやすい。

structure.label: M
structure.content: and it's easily confused for more common cardiac conditions

sentences.text: Fortunately, the initial treatment for takotsubo cardiomyopathy and a heart attack is the same.
sentences.translation_ja: 幸い、たこつぼ型心筋症と心臓発作の初期治療は同じだ。

structure.label: S
structure.content: the initial treatment for takotsubo cardiomyopathy and a heart attack

tokens.surface: for takotsubo cardiomyopathy and a heart attack is the same.

sentences.text: Doctors may prescribe blood thinners to prevent clotting in the heart and beta blockers to slow the heart's contractions.
sentences.translation_ja: 医師は心臓内での凝固を防ぐ血液希釈剤と、心拍を遅くするβ遮断薬を処方しうる。

structure.label: O
structure.content: blood thinners to prevent clotting in the heart and beta blockers to slow the heart's contractions

sentences.text: Beyond that, patients might take blood thinners and blood pressure medications to protect their heart in the long run.
sentences.translation_ja: それを超えて、患者は長期的に心臓を守るため血液希釈剤や血圧薬を服用することもある。

structure.label: O
structure.content: blood thinners and blood pressure medications

structure.label: M
structure.content: to protect their heart in the long run

sentences.text: But there currently aren't any specific treatments for takotsubo cardiomyopathy.
sentences.translation_ja: ただし現状、たこつぼ型心筋症に特化した治療法はない。

structure.label: C
structure.content: any specific treatments for takotsubo cardiomyopathy

sentences.text: We also don't know exactly when it will strike.
sentences.translation_ja: また、いつ発症するかも正確には分かっていない。

sentences.text: The population that appears most at risk is postmenopausal women, perhaps because estrogen helps prevent cardiovascular disease, and menopause decreases estrogen production.
sentences.translation_ja: 最もリスクが高いとされるのは閉経後女性で、エストロゲンが心血管疾患予防に働き、閉経でその産生が低下するためかもしれない。

structure.label: S
structure.content: The population that appears most at risk

structure.label: M
structure.content: perhaps because estrogen helps prevent cardiovascular disease, and menopause decreases estrogen production

sentences.text: But obviously anyone can experience traumatic emotions.
sentences.translation_ja: しかし当然、誰もがトラウマ的感情を経験しうる。

sentences.text: The good news is that in most cases patients make a full recovery.
sentences.translation_ja: 良い知らせは、ほとんどの場合、患者は完全に回復することだ。

structure.label: C
structure.content: that in most cases patients make a full recovery

tokens.surface: The good news is that in most cases

sentences.text: Because while our bodies are sensitive to the ups and downs of our emotions, they're also resilient.
sentences.translation_ja: なぜなら、私たちの身体は感情の浮き沈みに敏感ではあるが、回復力もまた備えているからだ。

structure.label: M
structure.content: while our bodies are sensitive to the ups and downs of our emotions

sentences.text: And much like a broken bone, even a broken heart can heal with time and care.
sentences.translation_ja: そして折れた骨と同じく、傷ついた心も時間と労りで癒えうる。

dysregulate.meaning: 調節異常を起こさせる

after_word.meaning: name X after Y で「Y にちなんで X と名付ける」

broken_heart_syndrome.literal: ブロークンハート症候群
broken_heart_syndrome.meaning: ブロークンハート症候群

widowhood_effect.meaning: 配偶者死後の残された側の死亡率上昇現象
